title: Status Asthmaticus
chief_complaint: Severe asthma attack. My inhaler isn't helping at all. I can barely talk. I've been in the ICU twice before for asthma.

physical_exam.General: Sitting upright, using accessory muscles, speaks in single words only, diaphoretic
physical_exam.Lungs: Severely decreased breath sounds bilaterally (SILENT CHEST). Prolonged expiration. No wheezing audible (ominous - severe obstruction).

hidden_data.abg: pH 7.28, PaCO2 48, PaO2 55, HCO3 22, SaO2 86%. Rising PaCO2 is OMEN of impending respiratory failure.

correct_diagnosis: Status Asthmaticus - Life-Threatening (Silent Chest, PEFR 25%, Rising PaCO2)
key_learning_points: Status asthmaticus = severe asthma unresponsive to initial beta-agonist therapy. LIFE-THREATENING features: Silent chest (too obstructed to wheeze), PEFR <33%, PaCO2 normal or high (normally low in asthma - hyperventilation), Cyanosis, Exhaustion, Altered consciousness
Continuous beta-agonists: Albuterol 10-20mg/hr continuous nebulization or 4-8 puffs q10-15min via MDI with spacer. Ipratropium 0.5mg q20min ×3 doses in first hour
Systemic steroids: Methylprednisolone 2mg/kg IV load then 1mg/kg q6h (or Prednisone 2mg/kg PO). Dexamethasone alternative
Magnesium sulfate 50mg/kg IV over 20min (max 2g). Bronchodilates and reduces inflammation. Second-line after beta-agonists

Heliox (70:30 helium:oxygen) reduces airway resistance if FiO2 <40%. IV Terbutaline or Aminophylline for refractory (PICU, cardiac monitoring). NIPPV (BiPAP) can reduce work of breathing and delay intubation

INTUBATION: Last resort! Indications: Cardiorespiratory arrest, Severe hypoxemia despite max therapy, Altered mental status, Exhaustion with rising PaCO2. Ketamine is ideal induction agent (bronchodilator). Ventilation: Low tidal volumes (6-8mL/kg), low rate (8-10), prolonged expiration (I:E 1:4-5), permissive hypercapnia (pH >7.15). HIGH risk of barotrauma and hypotension post-intubation
